nuggets.headline: Kendrick Lamar’s journey to rap royalty began with a love for poetry
nuggets.text: Before Kendrick Lamar was crafting intricate raps, he was a poet. Growing up in Compton, he found solace and expression through writing, a passion ignited by his seventh-grade teacher. This early dedication to poetry in the midst of navigating violence and socioeconomic challenges laid the essential groundwork for his distinctive, narrative-driven lyrical style that we hear today. He even graduated high school with a 4.0 GPA!

source.title: 10 Most Interesting Facts About Kendrick Lamar's Rise to Fame - The Most 10 Of Everything
source.quoteSnippet: Kendrick Lamar’s journey to hip-hop royalty began with a deep-rooted love for poetry and storytelling. As a child growing up in Compton, Lamar found solace and expression through writing, long before the beats and bars entered the picture. This early passion for poetry laid the foundation for his intricate lyrical style

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar's early love for poetry shaped his unique lyrical style.

nuggets.headline: the iconic beat for Kendrick Lamar's "HUMBLE." was originally meant for Gucci Mane
nuggets.text: Believe it or not, the powerful, minimalist piano-driven beat you're hearing right now on "HUMBLE." wasn't initially crafted for Kendrick Lamar. Producer Mike WiLL Made-It actually developed the beat with the intention of recording a song with Gucci Mane. But thankfully for us, the beat was later shown to Kendrick, he recorded on it, and was ultimately convinced to keep it for his groundbreaking album *DAMN.* instead of it ending up on Mike WiLL's debut album.

source.title: Humble (song)
source.quoteSnippet: The beat for 'Humble' was developed by Mike Will Made It, with the intention of recording the song with Gucci Mane. However, the song was later shown to Lamar. After recording, it was initially agreed upon that it would be released on Mike Will Made's debut album Ransom 2, but others convinced Lamar to keep it for his upcoming album.

nuggets.imageCaption: The striking visual for Kendrick Lamar's chart-topping single "HUMBLE."

nuggets.headline: if you love Kendrick Lamar, you need to hear fellow Black Hippy member ScHoolboy Q
nuggets.text: If you're digging the raw energy and lyrical depth of Kendrick Lamar, then you absolutely have to check out ScHoolboy Q. He's not just a similar artist; they're longtime collaborators and both members of the West Coast hip-hop collective Black Hippy, alongside Jay Rock and Ab-Soul. Their shared history and creative synergy mean you'll find plenty more to explore in ScHoolboy Q's discography.

source.title: Similar artists - Vince Staples | Last.fm
source.quoteSnippet: Quincey Matthew Hanley (born October 26, 1986), better known by his stage name ScHoolboy Q is an American recording hip hop artist from Los Angeles, California. Hanley is signed to Top Dawg Entertainment and is a member of Black Hippy along withJay Rock,Kendrick LamarandAb-Soul.

nuggets.imageCaption: ScHoolboy Q is a fellow member of the Black Hippy collective.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar, born in Compton, California, is celebrated as one of hip-hop's most profound storytellers. His albums like 'DAMN.' and 'To Pimp a Butterfly' aren't just critically acclaimed; they're cultural milestones known for deep lyrical resonance and innovative production.
